key_suppliers: ICBC Financial Services (clearing provider for U.S. government bonds)
supply_chain.dependencies: Reliance on third-party suppliers for key products and services including computers, equipment, software licenses, bond reference databases, clearing and settlement of Open Trading transactions, and technology underpinning key portions of MarketAxess Rates platform. Dependence on external data sources from competitors, clients, self-regulatory organizations, rating agencies and third-party data providers.
supply_chain.risks: Third-party vendors may not continue to provide services efficiently or cost-effectively. Vendors may not adequately expand services to meet needs or meet increasing regulatory requirements. Disruptions in third-party services (including cybersecurity incidents) could materially adversely affect business. Example: November 2023 ransomware attack on ICBC (clearing provider) required switching to different clearing arrangement. Competitors could revise terms or cease providing data/information services. Adverse changes in relationships with third-party service providers could have material adverse effect.

regulatory_risks: Extensive government and self-regulatory organization regulation in U.S. and internationally
SEC proposed rules to expand Regulation ATS and Regulation SCI to alternative trading systems trading government securities
SEC proposed amendment to definition of 'exchange' to include Communication Protocol Systems (RFQ protocols)
SEC final rules on central clearing of U.S. Treasury securities (effective December 31, 2025 for cash transactions, June 30, 2026 for repo)
MiFID II and MiFIR compliance requirements in EU and UK
MiFIR Review changes including consolidated tape framework, data quality standards, organizational requirements for APAs and ARMs
Digital Operational Resilience Act (DORA) applicable from January 2025 with ICT governance, risk management, resilience testing requirements
Brexit-related divergence of UK and EU legal/regulatory requirements increasing complexity and costs
GDPR and UK Data Protection regime compliance
Privacy and data protection laws across multiple jurisdictions
Regulatory capital requirements for broker-dealer subsidiaries
FCA regulation in UK, AFM and DNB regulation in Netherlands, ESMA regulation in EU
FINRA, MSRB, NFA, CFTC regulation in U.S.
Regulatory approval requirements for changes in control of subsidiaries
Increasing regulatory focus on cybersecurity governance and risk management
AI technology regulation evolving with potential intellectual property and compliance impacts
Potential new U.S. presidential administration policy changes affecting pending regulatory proposals

sanction_risks: Economic sanctions levied against states or individuals could expose company to significant operational and regulatory risks

February 2022 sanctions following Russia-Ukraine war prohibited transactions with Russian entities including major banks, limited transactions in Russian sovereign debt, constrained investment/trade/financing in certain Ukraine regions
Sanctions may limit ability to effect transactions in certain instruments on platforms
Expanded sanctions or ongoing war/geopolitical tensions may adversely affect global economy and platform participants
Sanctions may limit ability to effect transactions in certain instruments on platforms
